senses.definition: At times in the past; formerly (used mainly in old writing).
examples: Customs that were common aforetimes are now forgotten.
The village was richer aforetimes, before the mine closed.

senses.definition: Past periods of time (used mainly in old or literary English).
examples: In the aforetimes, the city was a great seaport.
Legends from the aforetimes still shape their beliefs.

senses.definition: Before now; previously (very rare and old use).
examples: As aforeward stated, the king refused their offer.
The matter had been discussed aforeward in council.

senses.definition: Used to say that if something is true, then another thing is even more certainly true.
examples: If he cannot manage a small team, he is a fortiori unfit to lead the whole company.
The first plan was risky, and the second was a fortiori unacceptable.

senses.definition: In a state of conflict or difficulty with someone or something.
examples: The company ran afoul of new environmental regulations.
He fell afoul of the local authorities after the protest.

senses.definition: Twisted together or caught so that movement is difficult, especially about ropes or lines.
examples: The fishing lines got afoul in the strong current.
The boat’s anchor chain was afoul of the buoy rope.

senses.definition: To set up or establish (older spelling of found; now obsolete).
examples: They afounde a chapel upon the hill.
A brotherhood was afounde in that year.

senses.definition: Feeling fear, worry, or anxiety about something that might happen.
examples: She is afraid of the dark.
He was afraid that he would miss the bus.
Many people are afraid to speak in public.

senses.definition: Used politely to say that you are sorry or that something may be true even if you do not like it.
examples: I’m afraid I can’t help you.
I’m afraid that’s not possible today.

senses.definition: The feeling of being afraid; fear (rare, non‑standard).
examples: Her sudden afraidness surprised her.
There was a deep afraidness in his voice.

senses.definition: Relating to African Americans (non‑standard spelling of African American).
examples: The book explores Aframerican culture in the 20th century.
Aframerican history is central to understanding the United States.

senses.definition: An African American person (non‑standard spelling).
examples: She is an Aframerican who grew up in Chicago.
Many Aframericans contributed to jazz music.

senses.definition: A name for a large region that includes Africa and Asia together.
examples: Some scholars study economic links across Afrasia.
The idea of Afrasia highlights shared histories between the two continents.

senses.definition: Relating to Afrasia or to both Africa and Asia.
examples: They discussed Afrasian trade routes.
The report focuses on Afrasian migration patterns.

senses.definition: Having both African and Asian ancestry.
examples: He comes from an Afrasian family.
Afrasian communities live in several coastal cities.

senses.definition: A person with both African and Asian ancestry.
examples: The city is home to many Afrasians.
As an Afrasian, she feels connected to two continents.

senses.definition: A noisy fight in a public place; a disturbance involving violence.
examples: Several people were injured in the afray outside the bar.
He was arrested for taking part in an afray.

senses.definition: In Arab and Islamic stories, an evil spirit or powerful demon.
examples: The tale warned children about an afreet that haunted the well.
An afreet appeared in the desert, offering him a dangerous wish.

senses.definition: Plural of afreet; evil spirits or powerful demons in Arab and Islamic stories.
examples: Legends tell of afreets living beneath the sea.
The village elders spoke of afreets that misled travelers.

senses.definition: In the open air, especially when eating or drinking outside (variant of al fresco).
examples: They chose to dine afresca on the terrace.
Coffee is best enjoyed afresca on a sunny morning.

senses.definition: From the beginning again; in a new way.
examples: After the mistake, they started the project afresh.
She decided to begin afresh in another country.

senses.definition: To trouble or worry; to make anxious (obsolete or dialect).
examples: Do not afret yourself over small matters.
She was much afret by the rumor.

senses.definition: To fret or wear away; to rub or eat away (obsolete).
examples: The waves did afrete the stones by the shore.
Rust had afrete the old gate.

senses.definition: Relating to Africa; African (archaic or literary).
examples: They studied Afric customs and beliefs.
An Afric landscape stretched before them.

senses.definition: The second largest continent, south of Europe and between the Atlantic and Indian oceans.
examples: She has always wanted to travel to Africa.
Africa is home to many different cultures and languages.

senses.definition: Relating to Africa or its people, languages, or cultures.
examples: They enjoyed African music at the festival.
African countries face many different challenges.

senses.definition: A person from Africa or of African origin.
examples: Africans live in many parts of the world.
As an African, he speaks several local languages.

senses.definition: Books, art, or other materials related to Africa or African peoples.
examples: The library holds an impressive collection of Africana.
He specializes in Africana from the 19th century.

senses.definition: Older spelling of Afrikaner; a white South African of mainly Dutch descent.
examples: The Africander farmers resisted the new laws.
She traced her family back to early Africanders.

senses.definition: A South African cattle breed with a red coat, used for meat and draught work.
examples: They keep a herd of Africander on the farm.
Africander cattle are known for their hardiness.

senses.definition: A belief, custom, word, or feature that comes from African cultures or languages.
examples: The novel includes many Africanisms in its dialogue.
Africanisms can be seen in music across the Americas.

senses.definition: A person who studies Africa, its languages, or its cultures in a serious way.
examples: She is an Africanist specializing in West African history.
Africanists gathered at the conference to share their research.

senses.definition: Relating to the academic study of Africa.
examples: The university has a strong Africanist program.
Africanist approaches highlight local voices.

senses.definition: The process of making something more African in character or control.
examples: After independence, many countries began Africanization of their civil services.
The Africanization of the curriculum was a key goal.

senses.definition: To make something more African in character, style, or control.
examples: They aimed to Africanize the school system after independence.
The festival tries to Africanize modern pop music.

senses.definition: Relating to people considered to have African physical features (technical, now often avoided).
examples: The skull was classified as Africanoid in early studies.
Such Africanoid labels are now questioned.

senses.definition: Plural of African; people from Africa or of African origin.
examples: Africans speak thousands of different languages.
Many Africans live in large cities.

senses.definition: A proposed scientific name for an early human or human‑like fossil from Africa (rare, technical).
examples: The fossil was once placed in the genus Africanthropus.
Africanthropus is no longer widely used in modern classifications.

senses.definition: A member of a Pashtun tribe living mainly in the mountains of Pakistan and Afghanistan.
examples: Historically, the Afridi controlled key passes through the mountains.
Afridi communities have a strong warrior tradition.

senses.definition: To frighten someone; to make someone afraid (old use).
examples: The sudden cry did afright the children.
He was afrighted by the storm.

senses.definition: A sudden feeling of fear (archaic).
examples: In his afright, he dropped the lantern.
An afright seized the crowd.

senses.definition: A language spoken mainly in South Africa and Namibia, developed from Dutch.
examples: She speaks both Afrikaans and English fluently.
Afrikaans is one of the official languages of South Africa.

senses.definition: Older spelling of Afrikaner; a white South African of mainly Dutch descent.
examples: The Afrikander farmers owned large estates.
Many Afrikanders spoke Afrikaans at home.

senses.definition: A South African cattle breed with a red coat, used for meat and draught work.
examples: They keep Afrikander on their ranch.
Afrikander cattle are well adapted to the local climate.

senses.definition: The community, culture, or collective identity of Afrikaners in South Africa.
examples: The party claimed to defend Afrikanderdom.
Language was central to the idea of Afrikanderdom.

senses.definition: Support for Afrikaner interests or culture; an expression or feature typical of Afrikaners.
examples: The speech was full of Afrikanderism and pride.
Some idioms in the language are clear Afrikanderisms.

senses.definition: A white South African whose first language is Afrikaans and whose family originally came from Europe, mainly the Netherlands.
examples: Many Afrikaners live in rural areas.
As an Afrikaner, he grew up speaking Afrikaans at home.

senses.definition: A South African cattle breed, usually red, known for its ability to live in hot, dry conditions.
examples: The Afrikaner is valued as a beef breed.
Afrikaner cattle are popular in arid regions.

senses.definition: Another spelling of afreet; an evil spirit or powerful demon in Arab and Islamic stories.
examples: The legend spoke of an afrit that guarded the treasure.
An afrit rose from the flames, whispering a curse.

senses.definition: Another spelling of afreet; an evil spirit or powerful demon in Arab and Islamic stories.
examples: An afrite appeared in his dream and threatened him.
Old tales warn travelers about afrîtes in the desert.

senses.definition: Plural of afrit; evil spirits or powerful demons in Arab and Islamic stories.
examples: The hero fought off the afrits with a magic sword.
Legends say the sea is home to many afrits.

senses.definition: A hairstyle with thick, tightly curled hair that grows out around the head in a round shape.
examples: He wore his hair in a big afro.
Her natural afro looked beautiful.

senses.definition: Relating to African culture or people, especially in style or influence.
examples: The band plays Afro jazz.
She loves Afro beats and dance.

senses.definition: A scientific name formerly used for parts of the African and surrounding regions of the Earth (rare, technical).
examples: Early biogeographers sometimes referred to the region as Afrogaea.
Afrogaea was proposed as a separate biogeographic realm.

senses.definition: Relating to Afrogaea, a proposed biogeographic region including much of Africa (rare, technical).
examples: The study focuses on Afrogaean mammals.
Afrogaean flora shows many unique patterns.

senses.definition: An insult or action that strongly offends someone (archaic spelling of affront).
examples: He took the remark as a serious afront.
Such behavior is an afront to our values.

senses.definition: To insult or offend someone (archaic spelling of affront).
examples: She felt afronted by his careless words.
They did not mean to afront their guests.

senses.definition: A tall African tree that produces hard, dark wood used in furniture, also called African teak.
examples: The table is made from afrormosia.
Afrormosia is valued for its durability and color.

senses.definition: Plural of afrormosia; trees of that species or pieces of their wood.
examples: Afrormosias grow naturally in West and Central Africa.
The carpenter used afrormosias for the floorboards.

senses.definition: Plural of afro; round, full hairstyles of tightly curled hair.
examples: In the 1970s, many people wore big afros.
Their natural afros looked stylish and bold.

senses.definition: To show a frown; to look displeased (non‑standard or dialect).
examples: He did afrown at the rude remark.
She afrowned when she read the letter.

senses.definition: A personal name used in some Muslim communities.
examples: Afshah presented her research at the conference.
They named their daughter Afshah.

senses.definition: A Turkic ethnic group living mainly in Iran and nearby countries.
examples: Afshar tribes moved across the region for centuries.
Afshar carpets are famous among collectors.

senses.definition: A family name, especially in Iran, Afghanistan, and surrounding areas.
examples: Nader Afshar founded a powerful dynasty in Iran.
Professor Afshar teaches at the university.

senses.definition: Towards or at the back end of a ship or aircraft.
examples: The crew moved aft during the storm.
Passengers seated aft felt the turbulence more.

senses.definition: Located at the back part of a ship or aircraft.
examples: The aft cabin is smaller.
They stored the luggage in the aft hold.
